title: rubus infestus subsp. drejeri
intro: Rubus infestus subsp. drejeri, also known as the wild rose, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Rubus infestus subsp. drejeri include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and berries to create a tincture.
